resources.string: Long click to select a folder
Alarms and timers using this sound will play the default sound instead.
The sound content cannot be accessed.
Your missed alarm has been deleted
Alarm set for less than 1 minute from now.
Alarm set for %1$s and %2$s from now.
Alarm set for %1$s and %3$s from now.
Alarm set for %2$s and %3$s from now.
Alarm set for %1$s, %2$s, and %3$s from now.
This widget is only configurable from Android 12
One or more essential permissions are denied. Not granting them can lead to malfunctions.
Click here to display the Permissions management page.
Set style and color applied to the application
Apply accent color to night mode
Displays a background for some application elements (alarms, cities, timer button, etc.)
Displays a background border for some application elements (alarms, cities, timer button, etc.)
Tab to display when opening the application
Only some interface buttons are affected (e.g.: the timer play/pause button or switches)
Display the title in the toolbar
Display the tab indicator in the bottom navigation menu
Enable fade transitions when switching screens
Note: The screen will always be on if a timer or stopwatch is running and the corresponding tab is displayed, regardless of the status of this setting
Use a custom volume for each alarm
Allows setting a different volume for each alarm in the expanded alarm view
Improves Bluetooth compatibility and prevents sound interruptions during repetition \n\nNote: Some ringtones may have slight audio glitches
Enable automatic routing of ringtones to Bluetooth devices
Only works if a Bluetooth device is connected
Connect a Bluetooth device to access this setting
Disconnect the Bluetooth device to access this setting
Enable vibrations when alarm is snoozed or dismissed
Double vibration when alarm is snoozed and single vibration when alarm is dismissed
Turn on back flash when alarm is triggered
Enable automatic deletion of occasional alarms by default
This setting can be changed for each alarm in the expanded alarm view
Device is set to total silence
Swipe to dismiss or snooze alarms
Enter a value between 0 and 24 hours
Enter a value between 0 and 59 minutes
Enter a value between 0 and 59 seconds
Minutes to add to the timer
Enter a value between 0 and 60 minutes
Enter a value between 0 and 59 seconds
Enjoy the fruits of your labor.
Androids are known to be fast, but not as fast as you!
Let\'s do the time warp again.
Just a jump to the left.
You have a palette for haste.
Set style, time zone and change device date and time
While traveling in an area where the time is different, add a clock for home
Are you sure you want to reset all app settings? \n\nNote: custom ringtones will not be reset.
Set style, default ringtone, volume, actions to dismiss or snooze alarms, first day of the week and reminder notification time
Swipe left to snooze or right to dismiss
Swipe left to snooze or right to dismiss and delete alarm
Swipe left or right to dismiss
Swipe left or right to dismiss and delete alarm
Click to dismiss and delete alarm
Set default ringtone, volume and timer sorting
Reset expired timers with volume buttons
Reset expired timers with power button
Flip the device to stop the alarm
Shake the device to stop the alarm
Default time to add to timer
Display a warning before deleting a timer
Are you sure you want to delete the \"%s\" timer?
Set style, color and font of the screensaver
Opens the main Android screensaver settings
No alarm scheduled for this time
%s alarm can\'t be dismissed yet, still more than 24 hours away

► Light, dark or system theme;

► Customizable screensaver;

► Customizable widgets;

► Support for tiles in quick settings (for Android 7+);

► Backup and restore application data (except custom ringtones);

► Material design and dynamic colors;

► Set the alarms to a specific date;

► Swipe to delete an alarm;

► Duplicate an alarm;

► Flip and shake action to dismiss/postpone alarm;

► Turn off/postpone the alarm with the power button or volume buttons;

► %s

To ensure that the application works properly, some of your device settings must be changed:

• BATTERY OPTIMIZATION;

• NOTIFICATIONS;

%s Depending on the Android version of your device, some of these settings are already optimized (e.g. notifications).

You can change them now or later in the permission management settings:
Are you sure you want to leave the application?
Manually update holiday and work schedule data
Set the URL for holiday and work schedule data
Enter the URL for the holiday and work schedule data
Set style, colors and font size of the widgets
Use default color for main clock
Display next alarm on the widget
Use default color for next alarm
Use default color for city clock
Use default color for city name
Maximum font size of main clock
\"Display cities on the widget\" must be disabled to access this setting
Use default color for \"Next alarm\" and \"No upcoming alarm\" titles
Custom color for \"Next alarm\" and \"No upcoming alarm\" titles
Use default color for the alarm title
Show the permissions necessary for the application to work properly
Are you sure you want to revoke this permission?

By default, the Android system configures all user-installed apps to be optimized to save battery life. 

While this is acceptable for many apps, this app is an alarm app and often causes issues if configured to optimize battery. Once enabled, the Android system can kill the app at any time and/or cancel alarms it has scheduled, which would adversely affect the app's operation. 

We recommend whitelisting this app from battery optimizations.

In addition to setting alarms, this app also has a timer and a stopwatch mode. \n\nIn order for notifications to appear correctly for all modules and especially for the alarm to be triggered, we recommend activating notifications for this application.
Since Android 14, in order for the alarm to be triggered when the screen is off, it\'s imperative that \"Full Screen Notifications\" permission is enabled.
On Xiaomi devices, you should manually enable the \"Show on Lock screen\" permission from the device settings in order for the application to work properly.
Backup or restore application data except custom ringtones
Do you want to backup or restore application data?
Use a custom snooze duration for each alarm
Custom alarm snooze durations will be reset.%s
Custom alarm volumes will be reset.%s
Display Dismiss button as soon as alarm is enabled
If disabled, the button will appear at the same time as the notification reminder
Enter a value from 0 to 10 minutes
Enable long press on the action button to create alarms with delay
If disabled, two buttons will be displayed to create alarms
Use a custom volume increase duration for each alarm
Custom alarm volume increase durations will be reset.%s
Use a custom alarm stop duration for each alarm
Custom alarm stop durations will be reset.%s
Add a personal note to cities
Tap on a city to add or edit its personal note
Use default color for main clock
Use default color for hour hand
Use default color for minute hand
Use default color for second hand
Use default color for next alarm
Use default color for city clock
Use default color for city name
Use default color for the alarm title
Maximum font size of the main clock
Use default color for \"Next alarm\" and \"No upcoming alarm\" titles
Color for \"Next alarm\" and \"No upcoming alarm\" titles
Use a custom missed alarm repeat count for each alarm
Custom missed alarm repeat count will be reset.%s
Use a custom vibration pattern for each alarm
Custom vibration pattern will be reset.%s
Apply a blur effect to the background image
Display active timers in compact mode
Allows you to round widget corners if your home screen doesn\'t support it
